tool.name: preg
tool.summary: scan protein sequences with deterministic bounded regular expressions

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `preg` searches protein sequence records with a bounded regular-expression model and reports overlapping forward hits in a stable table. Epithema v1 uses Rust-regex-compatible ASCII expressions rather than the full historical EMBOSS search language.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one local protein input and one regular-expression pattern. The subject may contain one or more records. The pattern must compile successfully, must not be empty, and must consume at least one residue.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The output is a stable table with one row per hit. Each row reports the source record, the searched pattern, 1-based inclusive coordinates, and the matched normalized sequence text.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema preg`. Validation currently covers overlapping regex hits through the Rust tool and service layers.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release is protein-only, forward-only, and bounded to Rust-regex-compatible ASCII expressions. It does not implement reverse scanning, database retrieval, or EMBOSS-era expression aliases beyond what the regex engine already supports.

artifacts.label: Preg FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed protein FASTA fixture used for bounded regex searching.

examples.title: Report overlapping protein regex hits
examples.description: Runs `preg` against a committed protein FASTA fixture and reports overlapping hits for the expression `MAM`.

expected_outputs.label: Protein regex hit table
expected_outputs.description: Two overlapping hits are reported in the first fixture record.
